mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains near the zero lower bound, equity discount rates stay compressed and the S&P 500 should trend upward over the next month as the market prices easier financing conditions. The signal is invalid if rate cuts coincide with deteriorating activity that overwhelms the valuation effect.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods of low or declining FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 20d returns more often than 50% of the time, or if rolling 3-year IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recessionary threshold signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and lower risk appetite, which should push S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment is still rising rather than merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE > 5.0% episodes generate positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both improve together, the signal indicates broad consumer demand supported by job creation, which should lift near-term equity returns as revenue expectations strengthen. The mechanism is not decomposable into either series alone because sales growth matters most when payroll expansion confirms it.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and PAYEMS does not outperform each series separately, or if positive 20d returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-expansion episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO indicates stronger industrial production and operating leverage in the real economy, which should support S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. The signal should fail when production gains are concentrated in a short-lived rebound that is not followed by higher sales or employment.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d returns are not more frequent after sustained INDPRO strength, or if the rolling 3-year IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below trend signals collapsing housing demand, weaker construction activity, and spillovers into consumer spending and credit-sensitive sectors, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is expected to be most informative when housing weakness is broad rather than seasonal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 5%
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST below-trend episodes do not coincide with negative 20d returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears after seasonality adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS stays very low, real policy rates are deeply negative and financial conditions remain stimulative, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when inflation is rapidly decelerating and the low policy rate is already fully priced.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high inflation relative to FEDFUNDS does not correspond to positive 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction term is not stable across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, the labor market is clearly tightening and household income expectations improve, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as cyclical optimism feeds through quickly. The signal is strongest early in expansions and weaker when employment gains are already fully reflected in prices.
failure_condition: Reject if rising PAYEMS with falling UNRATE fails to produce positive 5d returns in more than 55% of instances, or if the sign is not positive in the majority of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP slowdown after a prior expansion signals that aggregate demand is rolling over, which should eventually lower earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter. The mechanism becomes most visible when GDP weakens while labor and sales indicators do not accelerate enough to offset it.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative on a year-over-year basis
failure_condition: Reject if negative year-over-year GDPC1 growth does not precede negative 60d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the signal's rolling 3-year IC stays below 0.03.
